name: condensed-spectrum
tagline: Topological phase transitions and topological phases of matter in a condensed-mathematics paradigm
description: A dark, ultraviolet-and-indigo theme built for a six-part modular research program on condensed mathematics and topological phases of matter. The palette reads as three registers of the program itself: deep indigo-void for the profinite/condensed substrate, electric ultraviolet for the moduli stack of Hamiltonians (the object being built), and a spectral cyan reserved for the gapless discriminant and phase-boundary motifs (the locus being excluded). A quantized amber accent is held in reserve for the invertible/capstone material only.

motif.background: Faint Z^d lattice-dot grid at very low opacity over the void background, with a soft ultraviolet radial glow centered on the hero.
motif.gradientPhase: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--accent) 0%, var(--accent-secondary) 100%) — the 'phase transition' gradient, used on card top-edges and the hero spine diagram.
motif.capstone: Part VI (synthesis) is the only surface allowed to use --warning (quantized amber) as a border/glow accent, marking it as the invertible capstone.
